work.

Clinical learning translated into practical references, patient education, and structured reasoning.

● All patient-facing work is de-identified.

ProjectDisciplinePreview
01

Clinical reference

Emergency Medication Reference Cards

A rapid-reference study system for common emergency medications and nursing considerations.

Translates dense pharmacology into a format designed for quick, consistent review.
Medication safetyInformation designEmergency care
02

Pharmacology

Common ED Medication Sheet

A concise learning reference connecting indications, precautions, and nursing actions.

Demonstrates structured thinking and preparation for time-sensitive clinical settings.
PharmacologyPrioritizationClinical preparation
03

Patient education

Community Health Teaching Project

A prevention-centered teaching resource created for accessible community health education.

Applies plain-language communication to practical health promotion.
Health promotionPatient educationAccessibility
04

Patient education

Patient Safety Brochure

A plain-language resource designed to make essential safety guidance easier to act on.

Balances clinical accuracy with clear, patient-friendly communication.
SafetyHealth literacyVisual communication
05

Clinical reasoning

Pathophysiology Concept Review

A visual review connecting disease processes with relevant nursing implications.

Shows the progression from foundational science to bedside considerations.
PathophysiologyResearchClinical reasoning
06

Clinical reasoning

Patient Profile & Care Planning

A de-identified preview of structured assessment and care-planning work.

Demonstrates organization of assessment findings, priorities, and nursing interventions.
AssessmentCare planningClinical judgment
Preview only. No patient-identifying information is published.
07

Reflective practice

Global Health Reflection

A reflection on culturally responsive care, resource awareness, and service in Kenya.

Connects global clinical exposure with adaptable, dignity-centered care.
